lede: A look at August 7 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Jimmy Wales and David Cairns (politician born 1966), and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Chechnya
events.desc: The Chechnya-based Islamic International Brigade invades neighboring Dagestan.

events.title: 1998 United States embassy bombings
events.desc: Bombings at United States embassies in Dar es Salaam, Tanzania and Nairobi, Kenya kill approximately 212 people.

events.title: Ivory Coast
events.desc: Ivory Coast becomes independent from France.

events.title: IBM
events.desc: IBM dedicates the first program-controlled calculator, the Automatic Sequence Controlled Calculator (known best as the Harvard Mark I).

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The Battle of Guadalcanal begins as the United States Marines initiate the first American offensive of the war with landings on Guadalcanal and Tulagi in the Solomon Islands.

events.title: British Grand Prix
events.desc: The first British Grand Prix was held at Brooklands.

events.title: Alice Huyler Ramsey
events.desc: Alice Huyler Ramsey and three friends become the first women to complete a transcontinental auto trip, taking 59 days to travel from New York, New York to San Francisco, California.

events.title: Simón Bolívar
events.desc: Simón Bolívar triumphs over Spain in the Battle of Boyacá.

events.title: George Washington
events.desc: George Washington orders the creation of the Badge of Military Merit to honor soldiers wounded in battle. It is later renamed to the more poetic Purple Heart.

events.title: Ming dynasty
events.desc: The Ming dynasty Chinese military general Cao Qin stages a coup against the Tianshun Emperor.

births.name: David Cairns (politician born 1966)
births.line: Scottish laicised priest and politician, Minister of State for Scotland

births.name: Jean-Luc Dehaene
births.line: French-Belgian lawyer and politician, 63rd Prime Minister of Belgium

deaths.text: Jonathan P. Jackson, American bodyguard and kidnapper
Rabindranath Tagore, Indian author, poet, and playwright, Nobel Prize laureate
